dialogues: Look at the ones place. Interesting. They all end in 0 or 5. Every single one. That's the 5s pattern. Multiples of 5 always have 0 or 5 as the ones digit.
Now let's use that. Here's 3 times 5. I put the factors: 3 and 5. You find the product. Skip-count by 5s, three times. Place the product tile.
Right. 3 times 5 equals 15. Ends in 5. Fits the pattern.
